title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) model widely used quantify degree coupling coordinated development among multiple subsystems. Originating physics, extensively applied regional human–environment systems.

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: Given n normalized subsystem indicators \(U_i\), standard coupling degree \(C\) defined : \[ C = \left[ \frac{\prod_{=1}^{n} U_i}{\left( \frac{1}{n} \sum_{=1}^{n} U_i \right)^n} \right]^{\frac{1}{n}} \] Although widely used, formulation may suffer scale sensitivity -amplification effects due power structure. address issues, several formulations proposed modifying functional form coupling degree.

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: First, Wang et al. introduce formulation incorporates pairwise differences among subsystems normalizing relative magnitudes: \[ C = \sqrt{\left[1-\frac{\sum\limits_{>j,j=1}^{n-1} \sqrt{\left(U_i-U_j\right)^2}}{\sum_{m=1}^{n-1}m}\right] \times \left(\prod_{=1}^n \frac{U_i}{\text{max} U_i}\right)^{\frac{1}{n-1}}} \]

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: Alternatively, Fan et al. propose simplified structure derived variance-like dispersion, directly captures imbalance among subsystem indicators: \[ C = 1-2\sqrt{\frac{n\sum_{=1}^n U_i^2 - \left(\sum_{=1}^n U_i\right)^2}{n^2}} \]

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: overall development level defined : \[ T = \sum_{=1}^{n} \alpha_i U_i \] \(\alpha_i\) weights \(\sum \alpha_i = 1\).

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: coordination degree \(D\) integrates interaction \(C\) development \(T\): \[ D = \sqrt{C \times T} \] - \(C\) measures coupling degree - \(T\) measures development level - \(D\) reflects coordinated development

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: CCD framework can extended \(\text{metacoupling framework}^{[3,4]}\), distinguishing: Intracoupling Pericoupling Telecoupling enables cross-scale analysis coupling.

what: Install necessary packages and load data
title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: gzma dataset represents Data Social Space Quality Guangzhou Metropolitan Areas China (2010). contains multiple indicators describing urban social space conditions. particular, four key variables correspond subsystem scores, including population stability (PS_Score), educational level (EL_Score), occupational hierarchy (OH_Score), income level (IL_Score). Load gzma dataset sdsfun package: Normalize *_Score columns:

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
code: ccd_standard = coupling::ccd(dt) head(ccd_standard) ##           C         D ## 1 0.8051958 0.6237105 ## 2 0.8914577 0.5497290 ## 3 0.8999423 0.7134825 ## 4 0.9346128 0.7632959 ## 5 0.9440912 0.7944703 ## 6 0.9519923 0.7858001 ccd_wang = coupling::ccd(dt, method = "wang") head(ccd_wang) ##           C         D ## 1 0.4722274 0.4776480 ## 2 0.6211236 0.4588675 ## 3 0.5375842 0.5514412 ## 4 0.5861986 0.6045044 ## 5 0.6640321 0.6662930 ## 6 0.6319211 0.6402164 ccd_fan = coupling::ccd(dt, method = "fan") head(ccd_fan) ##           C         D ## 1 0.4593785 0.4711049 ## 2 0.7164548 0.4928249 ## 3 0.4915051 0.5272784 ## 4 0.5399620 0.5801746 ## 5 0.5951898 0.6308098 ## 6 0.5907777 0.6190239 ccd_df = do.call(cbind, list(ccd_standard, ccd_wang, ccd_fan)) names(ccd_df) = paste0(rep(c("standard", "wang", "fan"), each = 2),                        "_", rep(c("C", "D"), times = 3)) fig1 = ccd_df |>    sf::st_set_geometry(sf::st_geometry(gzma)) |>    tidyr::pivot_longer(-geometry, names_to = "var", values_to = "val") |>    dplyr::mutate(var = factor(var, levels = names(ccd_df))) |>    ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_sf(ggplot2::aes(fill = val), color = "grey40", lwd = 0.15) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn(     name = "degree",     colors = c("#ffffcc", "#d9f0a3", "#addd8e",                "#78c679", "#31a354", "#006837")   ) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~var, ncol = 2) +   ggplot2::theme_bw(base_family = "serif") +   ggplot2::theme(     panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank(),     axis.text = ggplot2::element_blank(),     axis.ticks = ggplot2::element_blank(),     strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),     legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16.5),     legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15)   ) fig1

title: Coupling Coordination Degree Model and Metacoupling Analysis
text: Due data limitations, focus peri-coupling based Queen contiguity structure. Pericoupling among neighboring units weighted using inverse distance scheme. calculation coupling coordination degree adopts formulation proposed \(\text{wang}^{[1]}\).
code: nb = sdsfun::spdep_nb(gzma) swm_peri = sdsfun::inverse_distance_swm(gzma) for (i in seq_len(nrow(gzma))) {   swm_peri[i, seq_len(nrow(gzma))[-nb[[i]]]] = 0 } swm_peri = apply(swm_peri, 1, \(.x) .x / sum(.x))  mc_wang = coupling::metacoupling(dt, swm_peri = swm_peri, method = "wang") head(mc_wang) ##     Intra_C   Intra_D    Peri_C    Peri_D Tele_C Tele_D ## 1 0.4722274 0.4776480 0.7414392 0.7507625      0      0 ## 2 0.6211236 0.4588675 0.6712215 0.5061883      0      0 ## 3 0.5375842 0.5514412 0.4539894 0.4644758      0      0 ## 4 0.5861986 0.6045044 0.6692548 0.6808108      0      0 ## 5 0.6640321 0.6662930 0.6880871 0.6870927      0      0 ## 6 0.6319211 0.6402164 0.6064480 0.6032699      0      0 fig2 = mc_wang |>    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c("Intra_C", "Intra_D", "Peri_C", "Peri_D"))) |>    sf::st_set_geometry(sf::st_geometry(gzma)) |>    tidyr::pivot_longer(-geometry, names_to = "var", values_to = "val") |>    dplyr::mutate(var = factor(var,                               levels = c("Intra_C", "Intra_D", "Peri_C", "Peri_D"))) |>    ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_sf(ggplot2::aes(fill = val), color = "grey40", lwd = 0.15) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn(     name = "degree",     colors = c("#ffffcc", "#d9f0a3", "#addd8e",                "#78c679", "#31a354", "#006837")   ) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~var, ncol = 2) +   ggplot2::theme_bw(base_family = "serif") +   ggplot2::theme(     panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank(),     axis.text = ggplot2::element_blank(),     axis.ticks = ggplot2::element_blank(),     strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),     legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16.5),     legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15)   ) fig2

what: coupling | Analysis of Coupling Coordination Degree
title: coupling | Analysis of Coupling Coordination Degree
text: Analysis Coupling Coordination Degree coupling R package coupling coordination analysis based coupling coordination degree (CCD) models. incorporates metacoupling frameworks characterize cross-scale linkages, flows, feedbacks within among coupled systems. Refer package documentation https://stscl.github.io/coupling/ detailed information.

title: coupling | Analysis of Coupling Coordination Degree
text: Install CRAN : Install binary version R-universe : Install source code GitHub :
code: install.packages("coupling", dependencies = TRUE) install.packages("coupling",                  repos = c("https://stscl.r-universe.dev",                            "https://cloud.r-project.org"),                  dependencies = TRUE) if (!requireNamespace("pak", quietly = TRUE)) {     install.packages("pak") } pak::pak("stscl/coupling", dependencies = TRUE)

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
text: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD)

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
code: ccd(data, weight = NULL, method = c("standard", "wang", "fan"), threads = 1)

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
text: data numeric matrix data.frame. Rows observations, columns indicators. weight Numeric vector indicator weights. Must length equal ncol(data). NULL, equal weights used. method Coupling model. One "standard", "wang", "fan". threads Number threads used computation.

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
text: data.frame : C: coupling degree D: coordination degree

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
text: Full model definitions formulas available : https://github.com/stscl/coupling/discussions/3

title: Coupling Coordination Degree (CCD) — ccd
text: Input values normalized [0, 1].

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
text: Metacoupling Analysis

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
code: metacoupling(   data,   swm_peri = NULL,   swm_tele = NULL,   weight = NULL,   method = c("standard", "wang", "fan"),   threads = 1 )

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
text: data numeric matrix data.frame. Rows observations, columns indicators. swm_peri numeric matrix representing peri (local) spatial weight matrix. Must square dimension equal nrow(data). NULL, zero matrix used. swm_tele numeric matrix representing tele (long-distance) spatial weight matrix. Must square dimension equal nrow(data). NULL, zero matrix used. weight Numeric vector indicator weights. Must length equal ncol(data). NULL, equal weights used. method Coupling model. One "standard", "wang", "fan". threads Number threads used computation.

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
text: data.frame : Intra_C: intra-system coupling degree Intra_D: intra-system coordination degree Peri_C: peri-coupling degree Peri_D: peri coordination degree Tele_C: tele-coupling degree Tele_D: tele coordination degree

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
text: Full model definitions formulas available : https://github.com/stscl/coupling/discussions/8

title: Metacoupling Analysis — metacoupling
text: Input values normalized [0, 1]. Spatial weight matrices typically symmetric.
